nodes.title: Clip Duration in Ltx Strides as Minimum Ltx Clip Length ( A Mod of 8 +1 ) Note this is in Mod 8s, keep in mind the actual clip length will have a "+1" frame

widgets_values:
EDIT: NVM , leaving this node here for documentation,
but we don't need it to blend.

What we need is to always load the last keyframe from the prior clip, and use that as the first frame for the current clip. 

We should always therefore save out keyframes. 

If a prior keyframe is non null we'll use it. 

Else we'll.. honesly for any frame other than zero we should fail.. but I could allow an override to continue with a freshly generated keyframe.

NOTE we'll have to add an extra set of frames at the end of the last clip to ensure we land on a mod8 +1 offset.

And NOTE: ROUGHLY.. 3 offsets is a second.. for a typical 24-30 fps. Possibly closer to 4 right if 30 fps but yeah.

Which means if VRAM limits you to 16 seconds, then you have about 48 of these mods you can process per clip.

nodes.title: Ltx Clip Stride Length in Frames ( keep in mind that ltx shifts this by 1, so while the length is still always 8, the  actual frames would be 9, 17, 25, ..etc )
Ltx Frame Interval Offset from It's Stride Length ( as modulus ) _ LTX's min frame lengths are shifted by 1 from the stride length.
Clip OVerlap With Prior in LTX Strides ( NOTE due to ltx's min clip length interval of 8 frames, we can't just overlap 1 frame )

widgets_values:
NOTE this next part will not work unless we always use the same global seed.

One option, is to, if you want to override a seed for a given portion of generation, add a switch and a local override that doesn't change the resulting file name. 

Then, add the original seed, as the file name, the full scene level seed, but add any local overrides, the node they pertain to ( add that as an input when suporting an override, maybe a node template ), and the override seed to the metadata.

NOTE:

TODO ideally we can list all, that match the prefix, and find the latest number generation. 

For now, we are hardcoded to teh first genration. If  you generated the prior clip twice this will not work, so delete the 00001 clip or archive it, 

and rename the one you want to use as 00001

nodes.title: Clip Offset From Source Media In Frames
Shift Backwards By Accumulated 1 Frame Overlap

widgets_values:
Each successive clip, shifts backwars so that its first frame can be set to the exact same frame as the prior clips last frame.

This means the (current clip offset) * 1 ; or you know just the current clip offset, is the amount of frames we have to shift backwards to ensure we are overlapping with the last frame of the prior clip, accounting for it's own backwardds shift as well.

So for clip 0, 0 frames, 0 offset.
For clip 1, 1 frame shift for overlap, the prior had zero offset, but the current has 1 offset
For clip 2, 1 frame shift for overlap, but the prior clip had 1 frame also so 2 frames.

But this works out such that the number of shifts is equal to the clip offset.

NOTE This works with our current design of, just run batches till you run out of footage. But if you were trying to produce a specific run length, then you have to be aware of these overlaps when determining how many clips there would be in total and how much excess in the last clip.

NOTE: We can't compensate for the run time by adding a fraem to the end because LTX, in the current version requires duration intervals of every 8th frame with a 1 frame shift. So we have to shift the start and retain duration, rather than extending the trailing clip to encompass the overlap. 

ALSO because we are overlapping, this has implications for stitching the video together that are not yet addressed.

We want to keep clip lengths at ltx supported durations until we are done working with all the data. 

Only then, can we prune redundant first frames all but the first clip. Or in your video editor of choice, you'll need to make sure all the clips overlap by 1 frame and trum the audio accordingly.

NOTE Dimensions  Must be a multiple fo 32.

And all source images and pose images must be at the same resolution because we are not doing any scaling elsewhere.

keep in mind that  we are 2x upscaling too so.. keep things small.

action: The soldier is visibly upset as he talks. Occasionally distracted he may glance around. But he's intent on getting his point across.

They are still separate frames, it's a video to video training. 

But they are lowe resolution than the ref image and perhaps more importantly the video latent being mutated, by that downscale factor.

WE are supposed to be using the sparse tracks.

This node is to avoid bad output results caused by excessively large input image sizes. Because when we input one image, we use the size of that input image.

The **TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus** will scale your input to 1024×104 pixels. We use the size of your first input image. This node is to avoid having an input image size that is too large (such as 3000×3000 pixels), which could bring bad results.

nodes.title: Note: KSampler settings
widgets_values:
You can test and find the best setting by yourself. The following table is for reference.

EDIT we've updated to the 8step lora and the 2011 model so these values are probably not right.

| Model            | Steps | CFG |
|---------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Offical             | 50               | 4.0               
| fp8_e4m3fn             | 20                | 2.5               |
| fp8_e4m3fn + 4steps LoRA    | 4               | 1.0               |
